subject: [CPS] Remove duplicate configuration
commit_message:
[CPS] Remove duplicate configuration

1. Introduced spring profiles to override default properties value
2. Added config.additional to provide an ability to add properties
   without changing chart

Issue-ID: CPS-320
Signed-off-by: Renu Kumari <renu.kumari@bell.ca>
Change-Id: If621ad7c5f69f3398a8eae54d5fa6c563cfcba22

Build Failed 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-commit-lint/1682/ : FAILURE

The Commit title must follow this pattern "[WHAT] A title"

WHAT can be a component name (AAI for example), DOC for documentation, COMMON for common, ...

The title must start with a capital letter (A in the example) and a space must be set between the end of the topic and the actual title: "] A title" ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-commit-lint/1682/ )

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Provided that you have passwords in you application.yaml I'd prefer that we keep it in OOM repo and remove it from jar file

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Provided that you have passwords in you application.yaml I'd prefer that we keep it in OOM repo and remove it from jar file

We do need passwords for the CPS to run but they are defined to use environment variable right now. Please have a look at application.yml (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=cps.git;a=blob;f=cps-application/src/main/resources/application.yml;h=1bcb496a8aa0d0049b2e056fc546d54119a7200f;hb=refs/heads/master). 
What other approaches do you suggest other than keeping application.yml only in OOM?

One approach can be to have an application.yml in jar without any password details and add another (application-helm.yaml) in the OOM which will contains password or anything that needs to be overridden. Then, we use "helm" spring profile when deploying it from OMM.
What is your view on this?
